perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI', the NDA no longer explicitly allows disclosure to authorized representatives like consultants or contractors. This creates a contradiction because later, the text says 'Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.' If representatives are no longer explicitly authorized to receive CI, it's unclear who 'representatives' refers to, creating an in-text contradiction and potential ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allows disclosure of Confidential Information (CI) to 'authorized representatives (i.e., temporary employees, consultants, and contractors) who need to know the CI'. The changed text removes this allowance, restricting disclosure only to 'employees'. The contradicted text states 'Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.' Since 'representatives' is no longer explicitly authorized to receive CI under the modified text, but the recipient is still responsible for their breaches, this creates a strong in-text contradiction as it's unclear who constitutes a 'representative' and whether the recipient is liable for breaches by consultants or contractors.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except as otherwise required by law', the obligation to return or destroy CI becomes absolute, conflicting with the later clause 'Recipient may disclose CI (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law...'. Now, Recipient must always return/destroy CI, even when legally obligated to disclose it under a court order, creating an irreconcilable contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for an exception to the return/destroy obligation ('except as otherwise required by law'). The changed text removes this exception, making the return/destroy obligation absolute. This directly contradicts the portion of the contradicted text that allows disclosure of CI when required by law (e.g., court order), as the Recipient is now required to destroy the CI even if legally obligated to disclose it. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Recipient cannot simultaneously destroy the CI and disclose it as legally required.

perturbation.original_text: Unless expressly set forth in this NDA, Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI. Each party agrees that Recipient (A) may independently develop and acquire software, hardware, and other technology fo r its own products, services, and processes, and that having access to Discloser’s CI does not prohibit Recipient from freely developing or acquiring similar products without obligation to Discloser; and (B) is free to use the residuals of CI for any purpo se, where “residuals” means that CI disclosed in non- tangible form that may be retained in the memories of representatives of Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless expressly set forth in this NDA, Recipient does not receive any license or intellectual property rights to Discloser’s CI. Each party agrees that Recipient (A) may independently develop and acquire software, hardware, and other technology fo r its own products, services, and processes, and that having access to Discloser’s CI does not prohibit Recipient from freely developing or acquiring similar products without obligation to Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and (B) is free to use the residuals of CI for any purpo se, where “residuals” means that CI disclosed in non- tangible form that may be retained in the memories of representatives of Recipient.', the explicit allowance for the Recipient to use 'residuals' of the CI is removed. The issue is that there's still a requirement within Section 2 where Recipient is forbidden to reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI, creating a contradiction in whether the knowledge gained can be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed the Recipient to use residuals of the CI. The changed text removes this allowance. The contradicted text states that the Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI. By removing the exception for 'residuals', the changed text now creates a direct contradiction: the Recipient is forbidden from using CI in any form (including residuals), but the original agreement allowed it. This creates a strong contradiction, as it's unclear whether the Recipient can use the knowledge gained.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this NDA upon 10 days ’ written notice . However, all CI provided under this NDA will remain subject to the terms of this NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this NDA upon 10 days ’ written notice .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'However, all CI provided under this NDA will remain subject to the terms of this NDA.', the contract removes the specification for CI to remain subject to the terms of the NDA even after termination. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the Confidentiality Obligations outlined in Section 2, regarding the handling of CI, are no longer explicitly enforced after termination, creating a potential legal gap and internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose. Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written cons ent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensured that CI remained subject to the NDA's terms even after termination. The changed text removes this provision, creating a contradiction with the Contradicted Text (Section 2), which outlines the recipient's obligations regarding CI. Without the original text's provision, Section 2's confidentiality obligations are no longer explicitly enforced after termination, leading to a potential legal gap and internal contradiction, thus satisfying the conditions for Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA contains the complete agreement between the parties as to the subject matter, and replaces and supersedes any other communications, or agreements, whether oral or written concerning the subje ct matter. Regardless of conflict of laws principles, this NDA is governed by and construed in accordance with the substantive laws in force in the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA contains the complete agreement between the parties as to the subject matter, and replaces and supersedes any other communications, or agreements, whether oral or written concerning the subje ct matter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Regardless of conflict of laws principles, this NDA is governed by and construed in accordance with the substantive laws in force in the State of California.', the governing law is now undefined. The first part of the sentence says 'This NDA contains the complete agreement between the parties as to the subject matter', meaning that everything has been clearly defined, but then removing the law that controls the contract creates ambiguity, as the legal jurisdiction is uncertain, and goes against the principle that the NDA has the complete agreement between the parties, as there is no jurisdiction to oversee the contract. This can make the document unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CI is provided “AS IS” and all warranties, express, implied, or otherwise are disclaimed. Any attempt to transfer all or part of a party’s rights or obligations under this NDA without the prior written consent of the other party will be null and void. This NDA may only be modified by mutual written agreement signed by authorized representatives of the parties. This NDA contains the complete agreement between the parties as to the subject matter, and replaces and supersedes any other communications, or agreements, whether oral or written concerning the subje ct matter. Regardless of conflict of laws principles, this NDA is governed by and construed in accordance with the substantive laws in force in the State of California. The parties will comply with all applicable export laws and regulations in their use and disclosure of CI. This NDA may be executed in counterparts and may be signed electronically or with handwritten signatures. The English lang uage version of this NDA will be the version used if any dispute arises. Agreement Number: 00782386 Page 2 of 2 CONFIDENTIAL 23 August 2019 3:39:12 AM IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this NDA through their authorized representatives as of the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the NDA is governed by the laws of California, regardless of conflict of laws principles. The changed text removes this clause, creating ambiguity about the governing law. The contradicted text includes the original clause specifying California law. By removing this clause in the changed text, a direct contradiction is created because the 'complete agreement' as claimed, is now missing a key element: the governing law. This makes the document internally inconsistent and aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction because essential language has been removed, which creates a contradiction with the full, complete agreement clause.
